evidence: QueryExecutor interface has 6 public methods; all are synchronous (Java, no async); parameter ordering is consistent: lemma, pattern/deprel, minLogDice, maxResults across findCollocations, executeDependencyPattern, executeDependencyPatternWithPos, executeSurfacePattern.
executeDependencyPattern and executeDependencyPatternWithPos differ only in a headPosConstraint parameter appended at the end — this is a standard Java overload pattern and is well-documented.

findCollocations vs executeSurfacePattern: both return List<WordSketchResult> but have different semantics (findCollocations prepends the lemma as a quoted token; executeSurfacePattern uses labeled positions). The distinction is documented in QueryExecutor Javadoc but the method names alone do not communicate the difference clearly.

REST API endpoints: GET /api/sketch/{lemma} and GET /api/sketch/{lemma}/dep both work, but /api/sketch/{lemma}/{relation} vs /api/sketch/{lemma}/dep/{deprel} uses path-based dispatch that is not documented in a machine-readable way (no OpenAPI, no route listing endpoint).
Exploration endpoints accept 'seeds' as the parameter name for both single-seed (?seeds=house) and multi-seed (?seeds=house,garden) — consistent naming.

GET /api/semantic-field takes 'seeds' but GET /api/semantic-field/examples takes 'adjective' and 'noun' separately — inconsistent: the comparison endpoint uses 'seeds' but the examples endpoint uses individual parameters. A developer familiar with the comparison endpoint expects 'seeds' on the examples endpoint.

GET /api/concordance/examples uses 'word1' and 'word2' while the semantic-field endpoints use 'seeds' for equivalent noun parameters — inconsistent naming for the same concept (headword/collocate noun).

POST /api/bcql has a body-JSON interface while all other endpoints use query parameters — this is intentional (documented: 'to avoid URL encoding issues') but creates a mixed sync/async-style inconsistency at the transport layer.
Parameter 'limit' is used in /api/bcql and /api/concordance/examples but 'top' is used in exploration endpoints — two different names for the same concept (max results count).
All public handler methods declare throws IOException — consistent error contract at the Java level.

strengths: QueryExecutor interface uses consistent parameter ordering (lemma, pattern, minLogDice, maxResults) across all overloads.
Exploration endpoints consistently use 'seeds' as the multi-word parameter name.
All handler methods return JSON with a 'status: ok' envelope — consistent response shape.
The POST vs GET split for /api/bcql is explicitly documented with a rationale comment.
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: Issues are parameter-naming inconsistencies across related endpoints (limit vs top, word1/word2 vs seeds/adjective/noun) and one semantic inconsistency (findCollocations vs executeSurfacePattern with near-identical signatures but different behaviours).
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The score is around 73: the Java API layer (QueryExecutor interface) is coherent and consistent. The HTTP REST surface has three naming inconsistencies (limit/top, word1/word2/seeds/adjective/noun) that a developer using the API would notice when switching between endpoints. These are real usability gaps but not critical bugs. The codebase is well above 60 because the response envelope shape is consistent and all endpoints are documented in WordSketchApiServer Javadoc.

issues.summary: Two different parameter names ('limit' vs 'top') are used for the max-results concept across sibling endpoints
issues.suggestion: Standardise on 'limit' across all endpoints (aligns with REST conventions and the more widely used name in this API). Update ExplorationHandlers to accept both 'limit' and 'top' with 'limit' taking precedence, and update API documentation. Add a deprecation note for 'top' in the response parameters field.

issues.summary: Concordance examples uses 'word1'/'word2' while semantic-field examples uses 'adjective'/'noun'; no endpoint uses 'seeds' for two nouns
evidence: Semantic-field comparison uses 'seeds' for the same noun concept
issues.suggestion: Rename the /api/concordance/examples parameters from 'word1'/'word2' to 'noun'/'collocate' (or 'head'/'collocate') to match the semantic-field vocabulary. Document the parameter names consistently in WordSketchApiServer's class Javadoc table.

root_causes: The concordance examples endpoint was built separately from the exploration endpoints and adopted generic parameter names (word1, word2, limit) before the exploration API settled on semantic names (seeds, top, adjective, noun).
possible_false_positives: POST vs GET for /api/bcql — intentional design, documented, not an incoherence
